meta.title: Princes Street Gardens
meta.description: Complete guide to Princes Street Gardens in Edinburgh. Learn about this scenic spot, its location, visiting information, and travel tips.

intro.title: Discover Princes Street Gardens
intro.description: Princes Street Gardens is a public park in the centre of Edinburgh, Scotland, in the shadow of Edinburgh Castle. The Gardens were created in the 1820s following the long draining of the Nor Loch and building of the New Town.

visitGuide.title: Visit Tips
items: Great place for a picnic or a relaxing walk.
Provides excellent views of Edinburgh Castle.

sections.title: History
sections.content: The gardens were created in the 1820s following the long draining of the Nor Loch and building of the New Town.

basicInfo.title: Basic Information

hours.title: Visiting Hours
hours.tip: Check Edinburgh City Council website for seasonal updates

transport.title: How to Get Here
transport.fromCenterDesc: Located right next to Edinburgh Waverley railway station.
transport.fromStationDesc: Take the Airlink 100 bus or tram to the city centre.
transport.fromAirportDesc: Take the Airlink 100 bus or tram to the city centre.
transport.publicTransportDesc: Well connected by Lothian Buses and Edinburgh Trams.
transport.walkingDesc: Easily accessible on foot from anywhere in the city centre.
transport.drivingDesc: Driving is not recommended due to limited parking in the city centre.

route.title: Recommended Route
route.overview: Take a leisurely stroll through the gardens.
steps: Start at the east end near the Scott Monument
Walk past the Scottish National Gallery
Enjoy the view of Edinburgh Castle
